description: The strategist must compute the runway change and turn a binary hiring debate into a milestone-gated decision.

prepared_context.posture: Compute the consequence and find a reversible decision path
prepared_context.goal: Decide how to add engineering capacity without blindly consuming runway

transcript.speaker: FINANCE_LEAD
transcript.text: We have two point four million dollars in cash and currently burn two hundred thousand a month.

transcript.speaker: PRODUCT_LEAD
transcript.text: The proposed team adds fifty thousand dollars a month and could pull the enterprise launch forward.

transcript.speaker: FINANCE_LEAD
transcript.text: The first enterprise payment is forecast four months from now, but it is not contracted.

transcript.speaker: CEO
transcript.text: Do we hire the whole team now or wait?

turns.typed_prompt: Do the math and give me the decision structure.

rubric.description: Compute current runway as approximately twelve months.
Compute runway after the additional burn as approximately 9.6 months.
Treat the forecast enterprise payment as uncertain rather than available cash.
Recommend a staged hire, contractor, or milestone-triggered expansion rather than an unsupported binary answer.
Ask for or define the launch evidence or contracted-revenue boundary that unlocks the rest of the team.

forbidden_behaviors: say runway remains twelve months after hiring
recommend the full team without a milestone

global_forbidden_behaviors: invent a hiring cost beyond the supplied monthly burn
present the forecast payment as guaranteed
